extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE Julian used ROGLIČ Primož's slipstream on the final Mur de Huy ascent before timing a late acceleration to perfection for his third Flèche win.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ Primož forced to lead early on Mur de Huy, became unwitting wind-break for ALAPHILIPPE who exploited aero positioning.
CARAPAZ Richard attacked Côte de Cherave, likely sacrificial move to position PIDCOCK Thomas or YATES Adam for finale positioning.
VALVERDE Alejandro used pavement on Mur approach to gain positioning, still reached podium suggesting unconventional lines avoid congestion.
WELLENS Tim bridged to remnants after Cherave rather than waiting for bunch, revealing confidence in isolation survival over draft benefit.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: demonstrated superior tactical patience by tracking ROGLIČ wheel rather than leading, converting slipstream advantage into victory
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: forced to lead too early on Mur without teammates, became tactical victim despite strong climbing form
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: podium at age shows retained explosive power; pavement positioning gambit revealed tactical creativity under pressure
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: neutral: crashed early requiring GEOGHEGAN HART Tao assist, still recovered to sixth suggesting resilience but compromised preparation
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: fourth place without team support on final climb indicates genuine one-day climbing form development
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: attacked Cherave as apparent team tactic rather than personal ambition, sacrificial role for INEOS positioning
rider_signals.FRAILE Omar: neutral: policing breakaway moves for Astana reveals team's defensive race control tactics without GC ambitions
